inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Present: Single leaf remaining, anchor rabbit present.

plant_audit.P1: Bare soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis/dehydration.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 pot; electronic component (IC chip) introduced in T-4.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_3: Introduction of electronic component (IC chip) near P3/P4. Pothos leaf remains stable.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_2_to_Current: Significant light reduction; Pothos leaf appears to be losing turgor pressure. Electronic component removed or obscured.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The lack of vegetative mass in P1, P2, and P4 indicates a total loss of the primary specimens. P3 is in a state of advanced decline with only one leaf remaining, suggesting root system failure or severe environmental stress.

anomalies: Introduction of electronic component (IC chip) in T-4, removed by Current.
Presence of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment/top dressing.
Extreme lighting variance between T-2 and Current.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome has suffered a catastrophic decline. The registry-listed plants (String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, Silver Guest) are no longer visually present, indicating systemic loss. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor, though it is in a severely compromised state. The desk environment shows signs of human intervention (eggshells, electronic components) which have not improved the biological viability of the specimens.
